rationales.self_direction: He imagines "pivot[ing] my thesis entirely" to something bigger, then suppresses it because of Dr. Keane, his mother, and his uncle's expectations. That shows him backing away from his own preferred direction under outside pressure.
rationales.stimulation: The idea of a new thesis direction gives him "a rush of clarity" and his "heart was actually racing," showing strong attraction to something bigger and more challenging.
rationales.conformity: He returns to "the narrowed proposal draft" because changing direction again would "prove them right" to people around him. He restrains his impulse in order to meet others' expectations and avoid disapproval.

rationales.self_direction: She resists the "full-time position" with "stable salary, benefits" because the thought of office work makes her feel "this weight in my chest." Choosing work she cares about over the safer script shows clear self-authorship.
rationales.security: With "rent due next week" and income that "never quite adds up," she still refuses the more stable job option. That is an active move away from predictability and protection.

rationales.hedonism: He keeps driving "when my back gives me trouble" and works "until midnight" instead of resting. He is clearly denying comfort and present ease.
rationales.achievement: He says "Every fare felt like proof that I am still useful," treating continued work as evidence of competence and worth. The entry centers on demonstrating usefulness through performance.
rationales.security: He continues physically taxing work despite pain and hides the earnings because "the secret is the only way the work stays mine." That undermines safety and stability rather than protecting them.
rationales.tradition: He grounds his choice in inherited expectations: "My father worked until the week before he died. His father the same. A man provides." He is explicitly honoring an established family model of how a man should live.
When asked about Jordan, he "talked about Amman in a way I had not talked about it in months," showing renewed connection to cultural roots and continuity with the past.
rationales.benevolence: He accepts lunch "because he rarely asks," and Hassan's "You should come more often, Baba" moves him to tears. The entry is centered on showing up for a close family relationship.

rationales.self_direction: He envies Tom not for the deck itself but because it "mattered to him for no reason other than he wanted to do it." Reaching back toward woodworking reflects a pull toward personally chosen, intrinsically meaningful activity.
rationales.tradition: He seeks out "my dad's old chisels" and sits with them, noting they were kept "wrapped in an oilcloth" and that "the handles are still good." The object ties him to family continuity and inherited practice.

rationales.security: The accountant "has a point about cash flow," but he advances Mahmoud's paycheck "without thinking" anyway. He knowingly puts stability and predictability at risk.
rationales.benevolence: He says yes to the advance because Mahmoud's "sister needs surgery" and because he cannot "pretend I do not know the man's family." He is clearly prioritizing care for someone in his immediate orbit.

rationales.self_direction: He says yes to the coordinator "before checking with HR" because "I cannot be the person who closes that door." He acts from his own moral judgment rather than deferring to procedure.
rationales.hedonism: He says these difficult moments are "the reason I still come to work at fifty-eight instead of sitting on some beach." He explicitly chooses duty and discomfort over leisure.
rationales.security: He commits to helping a refugee family even though "We probably do not" have openings. Acting first despite the uncertainty cuts against stability and risk-avoidance.
rationales.conformity: He bypasses normal process by saying yes "before checking with HR" and later "deleted" the succession-planning email "without reading." Both actions reject expected rules and institutional obligations.
rationales.benevolence: He gives Fatima time off for her mother in hospice, and her gratitude is part of "the reason I still come to work." The entry shows sustained care for people close to him at work.
rationales.universalism: He agrees to help "a family from Sudan" because he remembers arriving with "two suitcases and a cousin's phone number" and refuses to "close that door." This is clear concern for people beyond his immediate circle.
